memberdef.name: XHI_NUM_REGISTERS
memberdef.initializer: 25 /* Note that the register numbering * is not sequential, there are gaps */

memberdef.name: XHI_NUM_FRAME_BYTES
memberdef.initializer: 404 /* Number of bytes in a frame */

memberdef.name: XHI_NUM_FRAME_WORDS
memberdef.initializer: 101 /* Number of Words in a frame */

memberdef.name: XHI_NUM_WORDS_FRAME_INCL_NULL_FRAME
memberdef.initializer: 202 /* Num of Words in a frame read * from the device including * the NULL frame */

briefdescription.para: Generates a Type 1 packet header that reads back the requested Configuration register.

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the register to be read back.

simplesect.para: Type 1 packet header to read the specified register

briefdescription.para: Generates a Type 2 packet header that reads back the requested Configuration register.

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the register to be read back.

simplesect.para: Type 1 packet header to read the specified register

briefdescription.para: Generates a Type 1 packet header that writes to the requested Configuration register.

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the register to be written to.

simplesect.para: Type 1 packet header to write the specified register

briefdescription.para: Generates a Type 2 packet header that writes to the requested Configuration register.

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the register to be written to.

simplesect.para: Type 1 packet header to write the specified register

briefdescription.para: Generates a Type 1 packet header that is written to the Frame Address Register (FAR).

parameterdescription.para: - Address Block Type (CLB or BRAM address space)
- top (0) or bottom (1) half of device

simplesect.para: Type 1 packet header to write the FAR
We are retaining this Macro for Backwards compatibility Use the XHwIcap_SetupFar macro.
